name: Chit-Chat Chinese Vocabulary (中文)
description: Mandarin vocabulary from Chit-Chat Chinese (Rachel Meyer, Fu Kuo-Chung, Huang Hsiao-Chien; Far East Book Co., 2010). Hand-entered as a study technique.

field_values.value: very good; fine (as in response to "How are you?")
wǒ yě hěn gāoxìng rènshí nǐ
"No it isn't." (said to a compliment) [lit. "Where where."]
"No it isn't." (said to a compliment; less formal, more used than the other) [lit. "Don't have, don't have."]
to have; to exist; to possess
to know (a skill); to be able to
to think (about); to want; to miss (somebody)
"I don't know."/"I don't have that knowledge"
to be embarrassed about (verb); embarrassing (adjective) [lit. not good meaning]
husband [Taiwan/Hong Kong?]; sir; Mr.; waiter (some places)
Miss [lit. little older sister]; prostitute (some places/contexts--be careful)
准备好了吗 好了 没有
